theorem does_the_series_n  : Does the series $ \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{\left(\frac{2}{3} + \frac{1}{3}\sin n\right)^n}{n} converge? After computing approximately 10^7 terms, the partial sums approximate 2.163$. See https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.11017 for a proof of the convergence, relying on an irrationality measure := by
